strategic_objective: Characterize the generalizability and stability of class-conditioned variation in backward packet length features under class imbalance and sampling biases.
key_checks: Compare class-conditioned distribution profiles of backward packet length features across multiple partitions
Measure variance ratios and unique value counts controlling for class imbalance
Assess sensitivity of these features to sampling bias or dataset partitioning
Evaluate local label-topology consistency around these features within each class
success_criteria: Evidence that class-conditioned variation is consistent beyond the current partition
Identification of features with stable versus unstable discriminative signals
Bounded understanding of how class imbalance and sampling affect feature structural profiles
router_constraints: preserve feature scope for backward packet length features
preserve class label information and partition metadata
